item.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani discusses American exceptionalism and "forces of division" in America 250 speech
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani gave a speech sitting behind the George Washington desk at City Hall on Friday morning to mark America's 250th birthday.

item.title: Venice mayor proposes new dynamic pricing tourist fee
item.description: The mayor of Venice says the city is seeking government approval to introduce a form of dynamic pricing to deal with tourism costs.

item.title: U.S. says it's monitoring oil markets for price-fixing, urges states to do same
item.description: Antitrust regulators suggested that state attorneys general could assist in investigating unlawful conduct by companies.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 5)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Reagan Washington National Airport pausing operations amid America 250 events
item.description: On Friday, Reagan Washington National Airport is pausing operations for about three hours due to rehearsals for July Fourth America 250 celebrations. On Saturday, DCA will halt operations starting at noon. The airlines and the FAA worked on the plan and any cancellations were made months ago.

item.title: Bessent on Trump accounts, gas prices and wage growth
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent spoke exclusively with Kelly O'Grady about Trump accounts, which officially launch on Saturday, as well as when gas prices could lower to pre-Iran war levels and wage growth for Americans.

item.title: Peace talks paused as Iran gears up for dayslong funeral for slain supreme leader
item.description: The flag-draped casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was put on display in Tehran Friday with millions expected to attend his dayslong funeral.

item.title: Meet the members of The U.S. Army Brass Quintet
item.description: The U.S. Army Brass Quintet joins "CBS Mornings" to celebrate the nation's 250th birthday. Master Sgt. Kevin Gebo shares the band's favorite songs to play and discusses what they're looking forward to most this weekend.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding day arrives with MSG under tight security
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to welcome around 1,000 guests at Madison Square Garden to celebrate their wedding, sources say.

item.title: Ken Burns says Americans are not as divided today as we were in history
item.description: Acclaimed documentary filmmaker Ken Burns reflects on the 250th birthday of America, political divisions in the country and how we can come together.

item.title: What to expect for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
item.description: Bryan West, the Taylor Swift reporter for USA Today, talks about expectations for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding and what it may look like inside Madison Square Garden. For the past week, clues have piled up and the iconic arena has been on lockdown for the couple's potential nuptials.

item.title: Actors bring historical figures and America's 250-year history to life in Philadelphia
item.description: Actors with Historic Philadelphia turn sightseeing in the city into storytelling - bringing historical figures like Ben Franklin and Betsy Ross to life. Janelle Burrell shows how they're celebrating 250 years of America.

item.title: Gov. Moore on if he'll run for president in 2028, the midterms and division in politics
item.description: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore reveals to "CBS Mornings" if he'll run for president in 2028 as he discusses the 250th anniversary of the U.S., division in politics and affordability.

item.title: Stars attend Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce rehearsal, but couple may already be married
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are having a wedding celebration on Friday at Madison Square Garden, law enforcement sources told CBS News. On Thursday, the couple hosted a rehearsal dinner with a star-studded guest list. Jo Ling Kent reports.

item.title: Behind the scenes of July Fourth fireworks show to celebrate America 250
item.description: The Fourth of July festivities in D.C. will include what's expected to be the largest fireworks show in American history. Pyrotecnico, which has been putting on displays for more than a century, is in charge of the celebration. Rocco Vitale, the company's president, gives a sneak peak at the operation.

item.title: View from Freedom 250 Ferris wheel
item.description: The Great American State Fair has taken over Washington's National Mall and includes a massive 110-foot-tall Ferris wheel. Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: The American innovation that made millions of others possible
item.description: From the lightbulb to the airplane, to medical breakthroughs and the internet age, the past 250 years have been defined by America's intrepid intellect.

item.title: Kevin McCarthy talks Trump finances, American's feelings on economy and future of country
item.description: Former Speaker of the House Kevin McCarthy discusses President Trump's approval rating on economic policies, his decision to not sign a bipartisan housing bill and the president's recent financial disclosures.

item.title: The monumental women who transformed America and how "We The Women" uncovers key historical records
item.description: CBS News special correspondent Norah O'Donnell and senior producer Julie Morse Goff join Lindsey Reiser to discuss their research for "We The Women," a collection of stories about American history as impacted by women pioneers from all facets of life.

item.title: Inside look at security operations for July Fourth celebrations in D.C.
item.description: Security is heightened around Washington, D.C., as crowds are set to gather to celebrate the nation's 250th birthday. Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: Eye Opener: Brutal heat wave drags into July Fourth holiday weekend
item.description: More than half of the U.S. is still experiencing intense temperatures as a brutal heat wave continues into the holiday weekend. Plus, America turns 250 years old this weekend. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: Mission launched to rescue a falling space telescope
item.description: Katalyst Space's LINK spacecraft is designed to capture and boost NASA's Swift observatory back to a safe altitude.

item.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Dangerous heat wave impacting much of the U.S. ahead of Fourth of July celebrations
item.description: An intense heat wave is sweeping across the East Coast as final preparations are underway for Fourth of July celebrations. Rob Marciano has a look at the forecast and its impact.

item.title: Ukrainian woman named suspect in Monaco bombing
item.description: The suspect was initially identified as a heavily built person appearing to be male, but Interpol now says it is looking for a 39-year-old Ukrainian woman.

item.title: What's open and closed on July 3 and July 4? See which stores are operating.
item.description: Major retail stores will be open on Friday, although some may have modified hours on Saturday, July 4.

item.title: "Vanishingly rare" copy of Declaration of Independence found in U.K.
item.description: A volunteer at the National Archives in London found the document among other papers seized from an American ship in 1776.

item.title: Security guard rescued from rubble 8 days after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Hernan Gil was brought out on a stretcher as elated rescuers cheered and hugged each other. He was loaded into a waiting ambulance and driven away.

item.title: 3 futurists predict what America will be like in the centuries ahead
item.description: From space to healthcare and artificial intelligence, what could the next 250 years of the United States look like?

item.title: 250 essential American songs to mark nation's birthday
item.description: What are the essential American songs? To mark the nation's 250th birthday, we asked that question to Sunday Morning's familiar faces, from performers to artists and writers to community leaders.
